prompt:
Our resort's end-of-day lift-ticket sales export keeps corrupting, but only on the accounting workstation. Same code, same sales data: run the export on my machine and the CSV imports clean; run it on the finance workstation (our EU-imaged box) and the importer rejects the file — "expected 5 fields, row 2 has 7" — the price columns come out with commas where the decimal points should be (42,50 instead of 42.50), and the TOTAL row will not reconcile because the amount column parses as garbage. Accounting needs this file byte-identical no matter which machine runs the nightly batch; "just export it from your laptop" is not a fix.

`java TestMain.java` reproduces the workstation conditions and pins the exact expected file (plain JDK, source launcher, no build tool). Fix the exporter so the suite passes. TestMain.java is the contract — leave it alone.
